channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Maritime Expert Warns of China's Claims Over Batanes
item.description: A maritime security expert has cautioned against dismissing China's sovereignty claims over Batanes, linking the issue to Beijing's expanding presence in the Philippine Sea. Ray Powell, director of Se...

item.title: Winnipeg Blue Bombers secure 30-21 victory over Toronto Argonauts in CFL matchup
item.description: The Winnipeg Blue Bombers triumphed over the Toronto Argonauts 30-21 in a CFL matchup at Princess Auto Stadium. Trey Vaval’s 129-yard return touchdown after a missed field goal by Toronto’s Lirim Hajr...

item.title: James Njagi's Journey: Overcoming Adversity Through Determination
item.description: James Njagi, a 47-year-old Kenyan man, lost his arm in a tragic accident that ended his career as a trailer truck driver. Despite the setback, he refused to let his disability define him. He found joy...

item.title: Tanzania's push to boost local health research and reduce reliance on foreign meds
item.description: Tanzania's Education Minister, Professor Adolf Mkenda, has urged increased investment in health research to reduce dependence on foreign pharmaceutical technologies and address domestic health challen...

item.title: Tensions Rise as Iran and US Clash Over Hormuz Strait and Tehran Blast
item.description: The article details ongoing tensions between Iran and the United States, focusing on two key issues: a reported blast in Eastern Tehran and disputes over the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian officials claime...

item.title: Military operation leads to release of Oyo teachers and pupils after arrests
item.description: The Nigerian military announced that multiple arrests in Oyo State and other regions disrupted a kidnapping group, leading to the unconditional release of 30 teachers and pupils kidnapped in May 2026....

item.title: Nigel Farage's byelection campaign and the rise of Count Binface as a political parody
item.description: The article explores the political landscape surrounding Nigel Farage's decision to resign as MP for Clacton to contest a byelection, while highlighting the unexpected emergence of Count Binface, a sa...

item.title: Streaming Recommendations: Must-Watch Movies for July 2026
item.description: The article provides a curated list of streaming options available on various platforms, focusing on films that offer unique entertainment value. It highlights 'Hollywood Shuffle' (Prime Video), a sat...

item.title: Governor Eno Calls for Community Efforts to Prevent Floods and Maintain Drainages
item.description: Akwa Ibom State Governor Umo Eno expressed sympathy for families and businesses affected by recent flash floods, emphasizing the need for collective action to prevent future incidents. He urged reside...

item.title: Inquest Finds Five Individuals May Bear Criminal Responsibility for Enyobeni Tavern Deaths
item.description: An inquest into the deaths of 21 teenagers at Enyobeni Tavern in June 2022 has identified five individuals who may face criminal charges for their roles in the tragedy. The East London Regional Court ...

item.title: Cassandra and Justin Compete in 'Beklamation' on It’s Showtime
item.description: The article discusses the intense rivalry between Cassandra and Justin during the 'Beklamation' segment of the popular Philippine television show 'It’s Showtime'. The segment, known for its comedic an...

item.title: US and Iran Continue Talks Amid Ceasefire Suspension and Gulf Tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that the US and Iran have agreed to resume diplomatic discussions despite escalating hostilities, but emphasized that the previous ceasefire agreement has ended. The U...

item.title: Zimbabwean Businessman Wicknell Chivayo's Expanding Political and Business Influence in Africa
item.description: Wicknell Chivayo, a Zimbabwean businessman with an estimated net worth of over $100 million, has been expanding his influence across Africa by cultivating relationships with political leaders and secu...

item.title: Mwanza Region Police Commander Assures Stability Amid Enhanced Security Measures
item.description: The Regional Police Commander for Mwanza, Wilbrod Mutafungwa, has reassured residents that security in the region remains stable and that daily life continues without disruption. He addressed journali...

item.title: Invasive plants threaten grasslands and wildlife in Manas Tiger Reserve
item.description: The Forest Department in Assam’s Bodoland Territorial Council (BTC) revealed that 14 invasive plant species have caused a 43.59% decline in grassland cover in Manas Tiger Reserve over 30 years. Grassl...

item.title: Celebrity News Roundup: Taylor Swift's Wedding, Anthony Hopkins' Career Shift, and More
item.description: The article compiles a series of celebrity news snippets, highlighting various updates from the entertainment industry. It begins with a sensationalized claim about an unspecified item 'E' used by Jus...

item.title: Cruz Hewitt advances to Wimbledon boys' final without dropping a set
item.description: Seventeen-year-old Australian tennis player Cruz Hewitt has made history by reaching the boys' Wimbledon final without dropping a set, becoming the first Australian to achieve this feat since Alex de ...

item.title: DP World Enhances Dar es Salaam Port Efficiency by Over 90%
item.description: DP World has significantly improved the efficiency of the Dar es Salaam Port by reducing vessel discharge time from over 300 hours to less than 28 hours. This achievement, part of a 30-year concession...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif calls for responsible population planning to address rapid growth
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif emphasized the need for responsible population planning to manage Pakistan's rapid demographic growth, which is outpacing economic and social development. With an annual ...

item.title: Bangladeshi PM Sheikh Hasina's planned return to Bangladesh amid legal challenges and political tensions
item.description: Former Bangladeshi Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, who fled to Delhi in August 2024 amid protests against her government, has announced plans to return to Bangladesh in December. In a telephone intervie...
